dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, cruel fate has dealt us a terrible hand. {quest_origin} brought {quest_threat_the} upon us, and now {quest_focus_the} {quest_focus_is} crumbling before our eyes.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: No one caused this on purpose. It is just bad luck, but {quest_stakes_the} will pay the price unless we act. The upheaval drew {enemy_type_plural} out of hiding {location_hint}. They are circling the settlement, and {quest_ally} says their numbers are growing.
dialogueChunks.outro: Kill the {enemy_type_plural} before they take advantage of our weakness. We did nothing wrong and yet we suffer.

dialogueChunks.intro: It was not an attack, {player_name}. Just bad timing and {quest_threat_the}. {quest_focus_The} gave way and now {quest_stakes_the} {quest_stakes_is} cut off with no way to reach us.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: To make matters worse, {enemy_type_plural} have moved into the gap {location_hint}. They are blocking the only path between us and {quest_stakes_the}. We need them cleared before {quest_time_pressure}, or those people will be stranded.
dialogueChunks.outro: Hunt down the {enemy_type_plural} and reopen the path. Those people cannot survive alone out there.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, {quest_threat_animate_the} from {quest_origin} scattered our supplies across the area. {quest_focus_The} {quest_focus_is} buried in the debris and {quest_stakes_the} {quest_stakes_is} at risk.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Scavengers are the least of it. The {enemy_type_plural} have been drawn to the disrupted area {location_hint}, and they are nesting in the wreckage. Nobody can get close to recover anything while those creatures are there.
dialogueChunks.outro: Clear the {enemy_type_plural} from the area so we can salvage what is left. You would earn {quest_reward_hint} for helping.

dialogueChunks.intro: You know what the cruelest part is, {player_name}? We did everything right. Planned carefully, built well. Then {quest_threat_the} hit and none of it mattered.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus_The} {quest_focus_was} fine yesterday. Today it is ruins, and the destruction has drawn {enemy_type_plural} from the surrounding wilds {location_hint}. {quest_ally} says the creatures sense easy prey. {quest_stakes_human_The} {quest_stakes_human_is} defenseless until those things are dealt with.
dialogueChunks.outro: Drive off the {enemy_type_plural} so our people can pick themselves up. Bad luck brought this: only action can fix it.

dialogueChunks.intro: Three things went wrong at once, {player_name}. Any one of them we could have handled. All three together? That is the kind of luck we have been having.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: First {quest_threat_the} hit {quest_focus_the}. Then the damage attracted {enemy_type_plural} {location_hint}. Then the creatures spooked our livestock and scattered them. Now {quest_stakes_the} {quest_stakes_is} in jeopardy and we cannot even begin recovery while those things prowl the perimeter.
dialogueChunks.outro: Kill the {enemy_type_plural} and break the chain of misfortune. {quest_ally} can handle the rest if the area is safe.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I would laugh at our luck if people were not suffering. Since {quest_origin}, nothing has gone our way.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_threat_The} weakened {quest_focus_the} just enough for the {enemy_type_plural} to move in {location_hint}. They have turned our misfortune into their territory. {quest_ally} tried to scare them off with fire, but there are too many. {quest_stakes_The} {quest_stakes_is} threatened as long as they remain.
dialogueChunks.outro: We need steel, not luck. Go out there and kill the {enemy_type_plural} before things get any worse.
